title: Carousel Magic V2 - Carousel JSON Schema
description: Schema for importing carousels into Carousel Magic V2. AI agents can generate this JSON to create carousels automatically.

themeId.description: Theme ID. Popular options: one-dark-pro, github-dark, dracula, monokai, nord, solarized-dark, tokyo-night, catppuccin-mocha, gruvbox-dark, rose-pine. See themes.ts for full list (56 themes).

fontSetId.description: Font set ID. Options: inter-jetbrains (default), poppins-fira, space-source, roboto-cascadia, dm-mono, geist, ibm-plex, outfit-commit, nunito-hack, plus-meslo

type.description: hook = title/intro slide, content = bullet points, code = code block with optional text, cta = call to action

title.description: Slide title. Use \n for line breaks. Used by hook, content, cta types.

body.description: Body text. Use \n for line breaks. Bullets: start lines with • or -. Rich text: **bold**, `code`. Used by content and cta types.

code.description: Code content (code slides only). Use \n for line breaks.

codeLang.description: Language for syntax highlighting. Options: javascript, typescript, python, csharp, java, go, rust, html, css, json, yaml, bash, sql, etc.

codeTextAbove.description: Text shown above the code block. Supports: # Heading, - bullet points, **bold**, `inline code`. Use \n for line breaks.

codeTextBelow.description: Text shown below the code block. Same formatting as codeTextAbove.

codeCaption.description: Small caption text below the code block filename

slides.title:
5 TypeScript Tips
You Need to Know
slides.subtitle: Level up your TS game

slides.code:
type IsString<T> = T extends string ? true : false;
type Result = IsString<'hello'>; // true
slides.codeTextAbove:
# 1. Conditional Types
- Check types at **compile time**
- Use `extends` keyword for type guards

slides.title: Why TypeScript?
slides.body:
• Catch bugs **before runtime**
• Better `IDE` support and autocomplete
• Self-documenting code
• Scales with your team
